one_liner: AAPL up 3.1% on 4.4x volume — a Selling Climax reversal signal — but DCF shows 31% downside to $196 fair value; the massive volume at a lower RSI (35) suggests buyers are trying to absorb supply from $283+ sellers.

news_catalyst.headline: Apple Raises Prices 15-25% on Mac and iPad Due to Soaring Memory Component Costs
news_catalyst.summary: AAPL is up 3.1% today on 4.42x average volume — the volume analysis flags this as a 'Selling Climax — possible reversal.' The counterintuitive bounce follows Wednesday's 6% selloff on Apple's forced product price increases of 15-25% (MacBook Air up $200, iPad Air up $150). Today's high volume with only a modest 3% recovery suggests sellers are still distributing at these levels. DCF base case of $196 implies the stock at $283 is 31% overvalued, consistent with Parkev Tatevosian's $200 fair value estimate.

technical.summary: Climactic 4.42x volume on a 3% bounce with RSI at 35.8 — the volume analysis interpretation is 'Selling Climax — possible reversal.' While this could mark a short-term low, the bearish MACD and no Wyckoff accumulation signals suggest this bounce is more likely a dead-cat reaction than a genuine reversal. Wait for volume to dry up and RSI to stabilize above 40 before considering entry. Support at $255.70; resistance at $315.

valuation.summary: 31% DCF downside to base case of $196. The bull case of $333 is near current price only in an optimistic scenario — not a favorable risk/reward. Apple's asset-light model is showing cracks as supplier leverage erodes in the AI era.

key_strengths: Unmatched brand loyalty and ecosystem lock-in
Services segment growing to 25%+ of revenue at higher margins
key_concerns: Forced 15-25% price increases will reduce device units and service attach rates
Supplier leverage eroded as hyperscalers displace Apple at TSMC and Micron
fundamentals.summary: Grade B fundamentals but the component cost inflation is a structural headwind, not a temporary one. Apple's outsourced supply chain is now a liability in an environment where AI demand makes Apple's suppliers prioritize data center customers.

risks: Price hikes will reduce device units and service revenue — double negative on earnings
4.42x volume Selling Climax may be exhaustion bounce, not sustainable reversal
Hyperscaler competition for TSMC/Micron supply is structural, not temporary
